name: Pasta Flora, Athens Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Using an electric mixer, beat the butter until light and fluffy.
2. Gradually add the sugar, 1 egg, and 1 egg yolk, then 3 tablespoons of Cognac. Beat thoroughly after each addition.
3. Sift together 2 cups flour, salt, and baking powder. Add to the batter slowly while beating on medium speed.
4. Remove beaters and finish mixing by hand, adding enough flour to form a soft dough. Knead the dough.
5. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
6. Meanwhile, slice apricots into uniform pieces and place in an enameled pan.
7. Soak figs and raisins in remaining Cognac until swollen, then mince and add to apricots along with orange rind, jam, and lemon juice. Stir into the apricot mixture and cook over medium heat until thick, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon.
8. Cool.
9. Remove dough from refrigerator and set aside one-third for the latticed top. Press larger portion of dough into a buttered 9 x 12 x 2-inch baking pan, pressing evenly about 1/4 inch up the sides.
10. Pour filling into dough-lined pan.
11. Divide remaining dough into walnut-sized balls. Roll each ball into 1/2-inch strips.
12. Using the strips, make a lattice over the top of the pastry. If using glaze, beat egg white slightly with fork and brush onto dough strips.
13. Bake in moderate oven (350°F) for 45 minutes, or until golden in color. Remove and cool in pan on rack.
14. Cut into 1-1/2-inch (or smaller) squares with a sharp knife.
Note: You may prefer to use peaches and peach jam, strawberries and strawberry jam, etc., instead of apricots. Diced candied peel adds a colorful note when substituting for figs. Slivered almonds can be added to the filling.
